tldr: Ensemble of ResNet and InceptionV3 models with a custom pooling layer combining attention-weighted average, max, and std pooling.
summary: The solution uses an ensemble of ten models including ResNet18, ResNet34, and InceptionV3 at different resolutions (1024x1024 and 768x768). The architecture modifies the last global average pooling layer to a concatenation of attention-weighted average, global max, and global std pooling calculated plane-wise. Data augmentation includes channel-independent contrast, rotation, scale, shear, and shift. A key performance boost came from identifying and averaging predictions for duplicate images in the test set.
extensive_summary:
## Architecture
The author replaced the standard global average pooling layer with a custom pooling block. This block concatenates attention-weighted average pooling, global max pooling, and global std pooling, where all statistics are calculated 2D-plane-wise. This is followed by two fully connected (fc) layers.

## Data and Augmentation
The input consists of RGB images supplemented with HPA external data. The training pipeline employs data augmentation including rotation, scale, shear, and shift, as well as contrast adjustments applied independently to each color channel.

## Ensemble and Training
The final submission was a weighted average of ten models:
- 3 x ResNet18 (1024x1024 resolution)
- 3 x ResNet34 (1024x1024 resolution)
- 2 x ResNet34 (768x768 resolution)
- 2 x InceptionV3 (768x768 resolution)

## Post-processing and Tricks
- **Duplicate Handling:** The author identified duplicates in the test set using pair-wise correlation on RGBY channels separately. Averaging the output probabilities of these duplicates provided a boost of 0.004-0.005 on the leaderboard (LB).
- **Thresholding:** The author experimented with thresholds, generally finding 0.2 to be effective, with some high-occurrence classes set to 0.3. To gamble on rare classes due to perceived leaks on the public LB, the author lowered thresholds for the five rarest classes to 0.1 and 0.05, though this ultimately decreased both public and private scores.
augmentations: contrast (applied independently on each color channel)
ensemble: Weighted average of ten models (ResNet18, ResNet34, and InceptionV3) across two different image resolutions.
post_processing: Averaging output probabilities of duplicate images in the test set and applying class-specific thresholds.
what_worked: Averaging the output probabilities of theduplicates in the test set
what_did_not_work: Lowering thresholds of the five rarest classes to 0.1 and 0.05
critical_findings: There are tons of duplicates in the test set.
